creator: Ram Iyer
title: (No AI) AgentMail raises $6M to build an email service for AI agents
dc:creator: Ram Iyer
content: AgentMail provides an API platform that lets you give AI agents their own email inboxes, with support for two-way conversations, parsing, threading, labeling, searching, and replying.
contentSnippet: AgentMail provides an API platform that lets you give AI agents their own email inboxes, with support for two-way conversations, parsing, threading, labeling, searching, and replying.

creator: Rebecca Szkutak
title: (No AI) Thinking Machines Lab inks massive compute deal with Nvidia
dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
content: The multi-year deal involves at least a gigawatt of compute power and also includes a strategic investment from Nvidia.
contentSnippet: The multi-year deal involves at least a gigawatt of compute power and also includes a strategic investment from Nvidia.

title: (No AI) Grammarly will keep using authors’ identities without permission unless they opt out
author: Sean Hollister
content:
![A screenshot of a draft Verge post in Google Docs with an AI-generated Grammarly comment using Nilay Patel’s name](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/grammr-crop.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Last week, my colleagues discovered that [Superhuman's Grammarly had turned me into an AI editor](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/890921/grammarly-ai-expert-reviews), using my real name, without ever asking my permission. They did the same to my boss Nilay Patel, my colleagues David Pierce and Tom Warren, and - as [_Wired_ initially reported](https://www.wired.com/story/grammarly-is-offering-expert-ai-reviews-from-your-favorite-authors-dead-or-alive/) last Wednesday - many authors far more famous than us. Grammarly's new "Expert Review" feature uses our names to give its AI suggestions credibility that they don't deserve.

Now, Grammarly has finally addressed the backlash - but not by apologizing, and not by walking the feature back. For now, it will graciously give us the chance to _opt-out_ of something we didn't know it …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/891822/grammarly-superhuman-expert-review-names-without-permission-opt-out-email)
contentSnippet:
Last week, my colleagues discovered that Superhuman's Grammarly had turned me into an AI editor, using my real name, without ever asking my permission. They did the same to my boss Nilay Patel, my colleagues David Pierce and Tom Warren, and - as Wired initially reported last Wednesday - many authors far more famous than us. Grammarly's new "Expert Review" feature uses our names to give its AI suggestions credibility that they don't deserve. 
Now, Grammarly has finally addressed the backlash - but not by apologizing, and not by walking the feature back. For now, it will graciously give us the chance to opt-out of something we didn't know it  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Last week, my colleagues discovered that Superhuman's Grammarly had turned me into an AI editor, using my real name, without ever asking my permission. They did the same to my boss Nilay Patel, my colleagues David Pierce and Tom Warren, and - as Wired initially reported last Wednesday - many authors far more famous than […]

title: (No AI) Meta acquires Moltbook, the Reddit-like network for AI agents
author: Emma Roth
content:
![Digital collage of various toy robots.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/03/STK414_AI_CVIRGINIA_C.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Meta is acquiring Moltbook, a Reddit-like platform where AI agents can make and comment on posts, [as first reported by _Axios_](https://www.axios.com/2026/03/10/meta-facebook-moltbook-agent-social-network). In a statement to _The Verge_, Meta spokesperson Matthew Tye confirmed the Moltbook team will join Meta Superintelligence Labs as the company looks for "new ways for AI agents to work for people and businesses."

Matt Schlict and Ben Parr launched Moltbook earlier this year, offering a "social" network for autonomous agents powered by the open-source AI assistant OpenClaw (formerly Moltbot). The platform went viral earlier this year for a number of posts - including [one that asks questions about AI consciousness](http://moltbook.com/post/6fe6491e-5e9c-4371-961d-f90c4d357d0f) - thoug …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/892178/meta-moltbook-acquisition-ai-agents)

Matt Schlict and Ben Parr launched Moltbook earlier this year, offering a "social" network for autonomous agents powered by the open-source AI assistant OpenClaw (formerly Moltbot). The platform went viral earlier this year for a number of posts - including one that asks questions about AI consciousness - thoug …

title: (No AI) OpenAI to acquire Promptfoo
content: OpenAI is acquiring Promptfoo, an AI security platform that helps enterprises identify and remediate vulnerabilities in AI systems during development.
contentSnippet: OpenAI is acquiring Promptfoo, an AI security platform that helps enterprises identify and remediate vulnerabilities in AI systems during development.

title: (No AI) How Descript enables multilingual video dubbing at scale
content: Descript uses OpenAI models to scale multilingual video dubbing, optimizing translations for both meaning and timing so dubbed speech sounds natural across languages.
contentSnippet: Descript uses OpenAI models to scale multilingual video dubbing, optimizing translations for both meaning and timing so dubbed speech sounds natural across languages.

creator: David Gilbert
title: (No AI) Fake AI Content About the Iran War Is All Over X
dc:creator: David Gilbert
content: X’s Grok is failing to accurately verify video footage from the Iran conflict and is sharing its own AI-generated images about the war.
contentSnippet: X’s Grok is failing to accurately verify video footage from the Iran conflict and is sharing its own AI-generated images about the war.

creator: Reece Rogers
title: (No AI) I Used Google’s New Gemini-Powered ‘Help Me Create’ Tool in Docs. It’s Great at Corporate-Speak
dc:creator: Reece Rogers
content: Google has injected Docs, Drive, Sheets, and Slides with its Gemini assistant, adding new features that can pull information from your emails and the web to help you draft content. I gave it a whirl.
contentSnippet: Google has injected Docs, Drive, Sheets, and Slides with its Gemini assistant, adding new features that can pull information from your emails and the web to help you draft content. I gave it a whirl.
